# contentful_app_key (Resource)

Manages a Contentful App Key.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "contentful_app_key" "this" {
  organization_id   = var.organization_id
  app_definition_id = var.app_definition_id
}

resource "contentful_app_key" "provided" {
  organization_id   = var.organization_id
  app_definition_id = var.app_definition_id

  jwk = {
    kid = var.key_kid
    x5c = var.x5c
    x5t = var.x5t
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `app_definition_id` (String) ID of the app definition for which the app key is created.
- `organization_id` (String) ID of the organization that owns the app.

### Optional

- `jwk` (Attributes) Public JSON Web Key for the app key. When omitted or null, Contentful generates a key pair and returns the private key once after creation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--jwk))
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the app key was created.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `key_kid` (String) Key identifier. This is the JWK kid and Contentful app key ID.
- `last_used_at` (String) Timestamp when the app key was last used.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) Private key returned by Contentful when the key pair is generated. This is only available immediately after creation.
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the app key was last updated.

<a id="nestedatt--jwk"></a>
### Nested Schema for `jwk`

Required:

- `kid` (String) JWK key identifier. Required when jwk is configured. This becomes the Contentful app key ID.
- `x5c` (List of String) JWK X.509 certificate chain for the public key. Required when jwk is configured.
- `x5t` (String) JWK X.509 certificate SHA-1 thumbprint. Required when jwk is configured.

Optional:

- `alg` (String) JWK algorithm. Defaults to RS256 when jwk is configured.
- `kty` (String) JWK key type. Defaults to RSA when jwk is configured.
- `use` (String) JWK public key use. Defaults to sig when jwk is configured.


<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Read operations occur during any refresh or planning operation when refresh is enabled.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  identity = {
    organization_id   = var.organization_id
    app_definition_id = var.app_definition_id
    key_kid           = var.key_kid
  }

  to = contentful_app_key.this
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `app_definition_id` (String)
- `key_kid` (String)
- `organization_id` (String)

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  id = "${var.organization_id}/${var.app_definition_id}/${var.key_kid}"
  to = contentful_app_key.this
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import contentful_app_key.this $CONTENTFUL_ORGANIZATION_ID/$CONTENTFUL_APP_DEFINITION_ID/$CONTENTFUL_APP_KEY_KID
```
